§6Upgrade Cards§f:
The §6Upgrade Cards §fare a §efamily of items §f that can be §binserted into §fAE2 blocks to §bmodify their behaviour§f. They are the §6primary way §f to customise §6storage capacity§f, §6crafting speed§f, and §6redstone control §f on AE2 blocks.

§6Card Family§f:
- §bBasic Card §f(appliedenergistics2:material:25) — the base card; required for §6advanced cards §f
- §bAdvanced Card §f(appliedenergistics2:material:28) — mid-tier card; prerequisite for §6Speed §f and §6crafting upgrades §f
- §bCapacity Card §f(appliedenergistics2:material:26) — increases §6storage tiers §f on a §6Storage Cell §f
- §bAcceleration Card §f(appliedenergistics2:material:30) — speeds up §6Import/Export Bus §f, §6Formation Plane §f, §6Molecular Assembler §f
- §bCrafting Card §f(appliedenergistics2:material:53) — required for §6Level Emitter §f autocraft trigger
- §bFuzzy Card §f(appliedenergistics2:material:27) — enables §6fuzzy mode §f on §6Storage Bus §f
- §bInverter Card §f(appliedenergistics2:material:29) — inverts §6priority §f or §6whitelist/blacklist §f
- §bPattern Expansion Card §f(appliedenergistics2:material:58) — adds §6pattern slots §f to an ME Pattern Provider
- §bRedstone Card §f(appliedenergistics2:material:31) — enables §6redstone control §f
- §bSpeed Card §f(appliedenergistics2:material:32) — increases §6crafting speed §f on a Molecular Assembler
- §bSticky Card §f(appliedenergistics2:material:33) — keeps §6items in slot §f when §6Inventory §f is full

§6Production Path (materials/basiccard.json)§f:
The §6Basic Card §frecipe is pattern "ab / cdb / ab":
- §ba§f: §6Gold Ingot §f(ingotGold) — 4
- §bb§f: §6Iron Ingot §f(ingotIron) — 2 (one in the centre top, one in the centre bottom)
- §cc§f: §6Redstone Dust §f(dustRedstone) — 1 (centre left)
- §cd§f: §6Calculation Processor §f(appliedenergistics2:material:23) — 1 (centre)
- Yields 2× Basic Card

The §6Advanced Card §frecipe (materials/advancedcard.json) is identical but uses §6Diamonds §f instead of §6Gold Ingots §f.

The §6Capacity Card §frecipe (materials/cardcapacity.json) is shapeless: §6Basic Card §f + §bany Certus Quartz crystal §f (covers #appliedenergistics2:certusCrystal).

The §6Speed Card §frecipe (materials/cardspeed.json) is shapeless: §6Advanced Card §f + §bany Fluix crystal §f (covers #appliedenergistics2:fluixCrystal).

§6Function§f:
- §bStorage Cell upgrades §f: Capacity, Fuzzy, Inverter Cards
- §bMolecular Assembler upgrades §f: Acceleration, Speed Cards
- §bInterface upgrades §f: Pattern Expansion, Acceleration Cards
- §bLevel Emitter upgrades §f: Crafting Card
- §bCable upgrades §f: Redstone Card (enables redstone control on the part)

§6Direct Descendants (TaskRetrieval graph)§f:
The jei-graph shows 4 items the Basic Card is a component of, plus a few dozen uses for §6advanced cards §f and §6specific upgrade cards §f. Common uses:
- §bMass storage §f: 6 Capacity Cards on a §6Storage Cell §f multiplies capacity
- §bHigh-throughput I/O §f: Acceleration Cards on §6Import/Export Buses §f
- §bCrafting patterns §f: Pattern Expansion Cards on a §6Pattern Provider §f

§6Practical Use§f:
- §eTip§f: keep a §6stock of Basic Cards §f in a §6ME Chest §f; they are the §bprerequisite §f for many upgrades
- §eTip§f: the §6Inverter Card §f is the §6most underrated §f card — pair with §6fuzzy mode §f for §6OreDict §f-aware item exclusion
- §cWarning§f: each card consumes §1 upgrade slot §f on the target block; most blocks support §b4-8 §f upgrades
- §cWarning§f: a §6Speed Card §f requires an §6Advanced Card §f installed first; always have both in stock
